description: Cross-platform keychain-backed env-var manager. Envchain-compatible plus a Windows backend.

platform.value_on_windows: Native Credential Manager backend — envchain has no Windows build; envvault fills the gap with the same CLI surface.
platform.value_on_unix: Drop-in replacement for envchain: Keychain on macOS, libsecret on Linux, identical bare-positional invocation.

prefer_default_when: shared team secrets — use Vault, Doppler, or AWS Secrets Manager
cross-user/machine decryptable secrets — use sops or age

options.description: Structured JSON metadata for AI agents
Store one or more KEYs in NAMESPACE. Values come from an interactive prompt (or --value for non-interactive; single key only when combined with --value).
With no NAMESPACE, list all namespaces. With a NAMESPACE, list its keys.
Print the value of KEY in NAMESPACE to stdout.

Allow --set to store an empty string. By default envvault refuses empty values because silently storing "" is the exact footgun the tool exists to prevent (child command fails hours later with 'invalid credentials').
Accepted for envchain compatibility (we never echo by default).
Defer decryption to a passphrase prompt. Accepted as a flag but not yet implemented — returns an error until v1.1.
Explicit value for --set (alternative to stdin prompting). Be careful with shell history.
Coloured output: auto (default when omitted), always, or never

exit_codes.description: Success; in exec form, the child process's exit code is passed through
Usage error: bad flags, missing positionals, mutually-exclusive action flags, or deferred feature used (--require-passphrase)
Runtime error: key store unavailable, permission denied launching child command
Not found: namespace or key missing on --get/--unset; command for exec form not on PATH

examples.description: Run gh with GitHub env-vars injected (envchain-compat form)
Prompt for and store two AWS keys in the 'aws' namespace
List keys stored in the 'github' namespace
Remove a key from a namespace
Inject GitHub and AWS env-vars (in order) and run deploy.sh
